operation: First, initialize a workplane on the front reference plane, then create a base rectangular box with dimensions 66 (X-axis length) × 10 (Y-axis length) × 70 (Z-axis length). Next, select the minimum-Y face of this box, create a new workplane centered on the bounding box center of the selected face, offset the sketch origin 0 units in the X direction and -30 units in the Z direction, draw a 66mm × 4mm rectangle, then extrude this rectangle 86mm outward along the Y axis to form the horizontal flange. Then select the top face of the newly created horizontal flange, create a workplane on this face, offset the sketch origin 0 units in X and 5 units in Y, draw a 50mm × 50mm construction rectangle, select all four vertices of this construction rectangle, and cut counterbore holes at each vertex with a 5mm through-hole diameter, 7.5mm counterbore diameter, and 2mm counterbore depth.
geometry: This is a solid L-shaped right-angle bracket formed by two mutually perpendicular rectangular plates joined seamlessly along one long shared edge. The taller vertical plate is narrower in depth, while the shorter horizontal flange protrudes outward from the lower section of the vertical plate. Four counterbore through holes are arranged symmetrically at the four corners of an unmarked centered rectangular area on the top surface of the horizontal flange, with the wider counterbore recess sitting flush with the flange's top face.
function: This is a heavy-duty right-angle mounting bracket designed for structural support and component fastening use. The counterbore holes on its horizontal flange allow flush installation of fasteners to fix the bracket to a flat surface, while its vertical face provides a stable mounting surface for supporting loads or attaching other components.
